title: theorematurgy
description: mod that throws you in most powerful magic system
body:
# Theorematurgy

**Theorematurgy** is a survival magic mod about reading local chaos, assembling axioms from researched verbalisms, and binding those laws into relics you can carry or wear.

This is not a spellbook full of fixed buttons. Every law is built from meaningful parts:

*   an **origin**
*   a **carrier**
*   a **path**
*   a **trigger**
*   an **effect**
*   an **ending**
*   optional **modifiers**
*   optional **stabilizers**

You study the place, compose the law, preview its cost, then decide whether it should be spoken by hand or sealed into matter.

## Core Loop

1.  Read the local chaos with the **Chaos Meter** or **Chaos Lectern**.
2.  Research new verbalism families through **archives**, **structures**, and **fieldwork**.
3.  Build axioms in the **Axiom Workbench**.
4.  Preview, utter, and refine them with the **Axiom Tablet**.
5.  Assemble and tune **Relics** with meaningful materials, cores, and forms.
6.  Wear relics in **Curios** slots for trigger-based lawcasting.

## What Makes It Different

### Chaos Is Local

Chaos is not just a mana bar. Every place has its own profile shaped by biome, altitude, weather, dimension, nearby materials, recent events, and more. A law that feels elegant in one location may be unstable somewhere else.

### Axioms Are Modular

You do not unlock “Fireball II” or “Ice Bolt III”. You unlock vocabulary. New research expands what kinds of laws you can write, not just how many times you can press the same button.

### Relics Matter

Relics are not disposable trinkets. Their form, materials, and core create a real attunement profile. A well-matched relic supports the right law more safely and efficiently; a mismatched one pushes strain and corruption faster.

### Exploration Has Meaning

Ruins, chambers, sanctums, resonance sites, cairns, and observatories are not just loot stops. They widen your language, teach new law patterns, and open rare archival formulas.

## Current Feature Set

*   Full chaos-profile system with local readings and server-authoritative previews
*   Playable axiom editor with a large verbalism library
*   Archive-driven research graph and formula recovery
*   Separate relic forms: ring, necklace, charm, and circlet
*   Multi-imprint relic loadouts with active imprint cycling
*   Curios integration for worn, condition-driven relic behavior
*   Patchouli guidebook with a full in-game progression route
*   JEI support for relic assembly and axiom editing workflows
*   Custom world sites and archive loot chains for survival progression

## Survival-Friendly Design

The practical route is readable and grounded:

*   craft a meter
*   study a place
*   build a law
*   preview it honestly
*   cast it by hand
*   then preserve it in a relic if it proves worth carrying

Theorematurgy rewards observation, composition, and preparation more than raw item hoarding.

## Requirements

*   Minecraft `1.21.1`
*   NeoForge
*   Curios API
*   Patchouli
*   Silent Gear
*   support: [boosty](https://boosty.to/strdsts)

## Optional Companion Support

*   JEI for recipe and editor assistance

## Short Description

Read local chaos, write your own laws, and bind them into relics. Theorematurgy is a survival magic mod built around modular axioms, meaningful materials, archive research, and world-aware casting instead of fixed spell buttons.
